name: Enchilada Casserole
recipeIngredient: 1 dozen corn tortillas, made from corn, lime, water only
2 cups cooked lentils (a little cooking water added for moistness)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook onions, garlic, and spices in ¼ cup red wine, broth, or balsamic vinegar until tender.
2. Mix all filling ingredients in a bowl.
3. Prepare a baking dish with a very light film of oil or cooking spray (8x11 inch or 9x13 inch, or a couple round casseroles that fit the tortillas).
4. Pour a small amount of sauce into pan and cover with tortillas, add a layer of filling, sprinkle with cheese, another layer of tortillas, pour on some sauce, add more filling and cheese, ending with a layer of tortillas and sauce topped with cheese.
5. Bake at 350°F for about 30 minutes.
Notes:
If you prefer to roll up individual enchiladas, this method works as well; however, I enjoy the convenience and simplicity of the casserole, which tastes just as good in my opinion.
